description: A globally renowned contemporary art district, a creative cultural park transformed from former industrial factories.
One of Beijing's most famous commercial streets, a century-old street blending traditional and modern commercial culture.
One of China's oldest surviving imperial gardens, with the White Pagoda as its iconic landmark.
A model of ancient Chinese imperial gardens, a UNESCO World Heritage Site centered around Kunming Lake and Longevity Hill.
A cultural and leisure district in Beijing, featuring creative boutiques, cafes, and specialty restaurants.
A historical and cultural district in Beijing, where hutong scenery blends with lakeside views, offering enchanting nightscapes.
A comprehensive showcase of Beijing's history and culture, a modern museum with a rich collection of cultural relics.
The highest point on Beijing's central axis, offering the best panoramic view of the Forbidden City, a Ming Dynasty imperial garden.
One of China's top science and engineering universities, with a beautiful campus and a strong academic atmosphere.
The main stadium of the 2008 Olympic Games, a masterpiece of modern architectural art and a symbol of Olympic spirit.
A commercial district with over 500 years of history, preserving the traditional hutong style and old Beijing commercial culture.
A famous hutong commercial street in Beijing, featuring unique shops and local delicacies, offering an experience of traditional courtyard culture.
The central square of Beijing, the capital of the People's Republic of China, and the largest city square in the world.
A famous venue for crosstalk performances in Beijing, housed in a traditional theater building, offering an authentic crosstalk cultural experience.
The Monument to the People's Heroes standing at the center of Tiananmen Square, a sacred memorial to revolutionary martyrs.
The largest tomb in the Ming Dynasty imperial tomb complex, the mausoleum of Emperor Yongle (Zhu Di).
A distinctive artistic hutong in Beijing, gathering creative boutiques and cafes, offering an experience of old Beijing hutong culture.
A fashion landmark in Beijing, a hub for international brands and a center of modern urban nightlife.
